steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content:
The roots are $x = 8$ and $x = -2$, so $f(x) = a(x-8)(x+2)$ for some integer $a > 1$. Expanding: $f(x) = ax^2 + a(2-8)x - 16a = ax^2 - 6ax - 16a$. This means $b = -6a$, so:
$$a + b = a + (-6a) = -5a$$
We need a value of $-5a$ where $a$ is an integer greater than 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '-10': $-5a = -10 \implies a = 2$. Integer greater than 1. ✓
  • Choice B — '-5': $-5a = -5 \implies a = 1$. Not greater than 1. ✗
  • Choice C — '6': $-5a = 6 \implies a = -\frac{6}{5}$. Not a positive integer. ✗
  • Choice D — '7': $-5a = 7 \implies a = -\frac{7}{5}$. Not a positive integer. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
